mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly change in UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series decline simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST change × RSAFS change) yields a Rank IC below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns do not differ significantly between joint contraction and mixed-signal periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production exceeds its 12-month moving average, supply-side bottlenecks amplify inflationary pressures, forcing the Fed to tighten policy and compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month average, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in post-2000 late-cycle episodes (accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth revisions show no correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive GDP surprises consistently fail to lift equity prices over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals an aggressive tightening stance that raises borrowing costs and suppresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold signal produces a win rate below 50% across all post-1990 tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between rising employment payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction that precedes a recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative Rank IC below -0.05, or SPX forward returns do not systematically decline during confirmed divergence episodes over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market downturn where housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, construction-related earnings drag and wealth effects suppress consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when HOUST falls below 1.2 million units, or the housing start threshold fails to predict negative equity returns in all post-2000 housing corrections (accuracy < 50%).
